BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin after landlord mentioned property taxes might bump the Oak Tree Rd shop rent. Pre-year-end review with Hari.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Revisit with Hari once 2025 actuals are closed. Shop nets ~$80k/yr.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $2,430/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $7,160 + $200 increase = $7,360. At $210 avg labor/job, $7,360 / $210 = 35.0 -> 35 jobs minimum.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $2,630/mo (unlikely per landlord)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Landlord said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is ~34-35 repair jobs/mo at current cost structure. At ~40 jobs we have headroom. Main concern is winter slowdown but even at 32 jobs (Dec low) we're safe.
ActionItems: Wait for landlord to confirm property tax situation
Revisit after year-end with Hari for proper modeling
